# html2canvas

**Version:** 1.4.1
**Source:** https://github.com/niklasvh/html2canvas
**Generated:** 2026-03-25

## Overview

html2canvas is a JavaScript library that renders DOM elements to a `<canvas>` element, effectively taking "screenshots" of web page content directly in the browser. It does not use the actual display but reads DOM properties and builds a canvas representation.

## Installation & Setup

```bash
npm install html2canvas
```

No Angular-specific setup required. It's a pure function-based library.

## Core Concepts

- Takes a DOM element as input, returns a Promise resolving to a `<canvas>` element.
- Reads DOM properties (computed styles, layout, images) -- does NOT capture pixels from the screen.
- Works entirely client-side; does NOT require a server.
- Cross-origin images require CORS or a proxy.

## API Reference

### Main Function

```typescript
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

html2canvas(element: HTMLElement, options?: Options): Promise<HTMLCanvasElement>
```

### Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| width | number | element width | Canvas width |
| height | number | element height | Canvas height |
| scale | number | window.devicePixelRatio | Rendering scale |
| backgroundColor | string \| null | '#ffffff' | Background color (null = transparent) |
| allowTaint | boolean | false | Allow cross-origin images to taint canvas |
| useCORS | boolean | false | Use CORS for cross-origin images |
| logging | boolean | true | Enable console logging |
| ignoreElements | (el) => boolean | - | Filter out elements |
| onclone | (doc) => void | - | Modify cloned DOM before render |
| proxy | string | null | Proxy URL for cross-origin images |
| imageTimeout | number | 15000 | Image load timeout (ms) |
| foreignObjectRendering | boolean | false | Use SVG foreignObject |
| removeContainer | boolean | true | Clean up temporary DOM |

### Exclude Elements

Use `data-html2canvas-ignore` attribute:

```html
<div data-html2canvas-ignore="true">Hidden from capture</div>
```

Or use `ignoreElements` callback:

```typescript
html2canvas(element, {
  ignoreElements: (el) => el.classList.contains('no-screenshot'),
});
```

## Common Usage Patterns

### Basic Screenshot

```typescript
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

async captureElement(element: HTMLElement): Promise<void> {
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element);
  document.body.appendChild(canvas);
}
```

### Download as Image

```typescript
async downloadScreenshot(element: HTMLElement): Promise<void> {
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element, { scale: 2 });
  const link = document.createElement('a');
  link.download = 'screenshot.png';
  link.href = canvas.toDataURL('image/png');
  link.click();
}
```

### Modify Cloned DOM Before Capture

```typescript
html2canvas(element, {
  onclone: (clonedDoc) => {
    clonedDoc.querySelectorAll('.edit-button').forEach(el => {
      (el as HTMLElement).style.display = 'none';
    });
  },
  scale: 2,
});
```

### Angular Component Integration

```typescript
import { Component, viewChild, ElementRef, afterNextRender } from '@angular/core';
import html2canvas from 'html2canvas';

@Component({
  template: `
    <div #captureArea>
      <p>Content to capture</p>
    </div>
    <button (click)="capture()">Capture</button>
    <div #result></div>
  `,
})
export class CaptureComponent {
  private captureArea = viewChild<ElementRef>('captureArea');
  private result = viewChild<ElementRef>('result');

  async capture(): Promise<void> {
    const canvas = await html2canvas(this.captureArea()!.nativeElement, { scale: 2 });
    this.result()!.nativeElement.appendChild(canvas);
  }
}
```

## SSR Considerations

html2canvas requires `document` and `window` objects. In Angular SSR:

```typescript
import { isPlatformBrowser, PLATFORM_ID } from '@angular/common';
import { inject } from '@angular/core';

private platformId = inject(PLATFORM_ID);

async capture(element: HTMLElement): Promise<void> {
  if (!isPlatformBrowser(this.platformId)) return;
  const html2canvas = (await import('html2canvas')).default;
  const canvas = await html2canvas(element);
  // ...
}
```

Use dynamic import to avoid SSR bundling issues.

## Error Handling

- Cross-origin images: Enable `useCORS: true` or use a proxy.
- Large DOMs: May be slow -- consider limiting scope to specific elements.
- CSS limitations: Some CSS properties (e.g., `backdrop-filter`, `mix-blend-mode`) may not render.

## Best Practices

- Always set `scale: 2` for high-DPI screenshots.
- Use `useCORS: true` when capturing elements with external images.
- Guard with `isPlatformBrowser` for SSR apps.
- Use dynamic `import('html2canvas')` to avoid SSR bundling.
- Use `ignoreElements` or `data-html2canvas-ignore` to exclude interactive controls.

## Required Manual Actions

None.
